description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized rock with a rough, weathered surface and a predominantly light brown to gray coloration. The rock features uneven contours, with one side appearing more angular and the other more rounded, suggesting natural erosion or breakage. Its surface is matte and granular, with visible patches of lighter and darker tones, possibly due to mineral deposits or exposure to the elements. There are small chips and crevices along the edges, and a faint yellowish mark is visible on one side, possibly from paint or lichen. The rock's overall form is asymmetrical, with a broad, flat base and a slightly sloping top. It resembles rocks commonly found in landscaped urban parks or as natural barriers along sidewalks and pathways.
description: A rough, light brown-gray rock with an irregular, weathered surface and angular edges, featuring a faint yellowish mark on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's broadest, flattest face with a rough, granular texture and subtle color variations.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the rock's sloping top and more angular side, with visible crevices and a slightly darker tone.
description_view_2: From this perspective, the rock appears thinner and more elongated, with the yellowish mark most visible near the center.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the rock's rounded end and the transition from the flat base to the more irregular upper surface.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or roadside barrier
